name: Harissa Lentil & Chickpea Shepherd's Pie
description: A smoky, harissa-led shepherd's pie with split red lentils and chickpeas in a rich tomato base, air-fried courgette and chestnut mushrooms folded through, and a 50/50 potato and sweet potato mash on top.

ingredients.name: floury potatoes, peeled and chunked
ingredients.notes: Maris Piper or King Edward recommended

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: for tossing the veg before air frying

ingredients.name: rose harissa
ingredients.notes: Belazu recommended. Start with 2 tbsp if your harissa is fiercer — taste before adding more; lower bound 2 tbsp per range convention

ingredients.name: fresh parsley, chopped
ingredients.notes: small handful; or fresh coriander, chopped

steps.title: Start the potatoes
steps.content: Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Put {0001} and {0002} into a large pan, cover with cold salted water, bring to the boil, then simmer until tender — around 18–20 minutes.

steps.title: Toss the veg
steps.content: In a bowl, toss {0007} and {0008} with {0009}, {0010}, {0011} and {0012} until everything is well coated.

steps.title: Air fry the veg
steps.content: Spread the courgettes and mushrooms into the air fryer basket — don't overcrowd; cook in two batches if needed. Cook at 200°C for 14 minutes, shaking halfway. You want golden edges on the courgette and mushrooms that are shrunk and glossy. Set aside.

steps.title: Sweat the onion
steps.content: While the veg are in the air fryer, heat {0013} in a wide, deep pan over medium-low heat. Add {0014} with a pinch of {0005} and cook gently until soft and translucent — around 8 minutes.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.content: Add {0015}, {0016}, {0017}, {0018} and {0019}. Stir constantly for about 90 seconds until everything smells fragrant and the harissa has darkened the oil.

steps.title: Reduce the tomatoes
steps.content: Add {0020} and turn the heat up. Let them bubble and reduce hard for about 5 minutes until the tomatoes thicken and turn a deeper, brick-red colour.

steps.title: Simmer the lentils
steps.content: Stir in {0021}, {0022} and {0023}. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover loosely, and cook for around 18 minutes until the lentils have broken down and the base is thick. Stir occasionally to stop it catching.

steps.title: Make the mash
steps.content: Drain {0001} and {0002}, return to the pan, and mash with {0003} and {0004}. Season generously with {0005} and {0006}. You want it firm enough to hold its shape on top of the filling.

steps.title: Finish the base
steps.content: Stir {0024} into the lentil base. Add {0025} and check seasoning — it should taste savoury and smoky with a quiet sharpness from the vinegar. Adjust {0005} and {0019} to taste.

steps.title: Fold in the air-fried veg
steps.content: Tip the air-fried courgettes and mushrooms into the base along with {0026}. Fold gently — you want them intact, not crushed. The filling should be thick and scoopable. If it looks too wet, simmer uncovered for a few more minutes.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.content: Transfer the filling into a baking dish (roughly 25 × 20cm). Spoon the mash on top in dollops, then spread to cover. Rough the surface up with a fork — those peaks will crisp up in the oven.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Bake at 200°C fan for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden and crisp and the filling is bubbling at the edges.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.content: Rest for 5 minutes before serving — it will firm up and the flavours settle. Serve with broccoli or cabbage on the side.

notes:
Improves overnight. Reheat individual portions at 180°C for 20 minutes covered, then uncovered for 5 minutes to re-crisp the top. Alternatively, assemble two smaller dishes and only bake one — the unbaked dish keeps better in the fridge.

If the filling looks too wet before the mash goes on, simmer uncovered for a few more minutes. If too thick, add a splash more stock.

Rose harissa varies by brand. 3 tbsp gives a warm, fragrant heat with Belazu; start with 2 tbsp if yours is fiercer.

Side suggestions: broccoli charred in the air fryer at 200°C for 8–10 minutes with olive oil, salt, and chilli; or cabbage shredded and fried hard with caraway or cumin seeds.

_fixture_notes.external_ref: Supplied slug 'harissa-lentil-chickpea-shepherds-pie' used verbatim; not re-derived.
_fixture_notes.dietary_category: Source labels dish 'Pescatarian-friendly and dairy-free'. All ingredients are plant-based — no fish, dairy, or eggs present. Least-restrictive-eater rule: classified 'vegan', not 'pescatarian'. 'Pescatarian-friendly' describes who can eat it, not what it contains.
_fixture_notes.dietary_restrictions: Soy: soy sauce (or tamari) and miso paste. Oat milk can contain gluten if not certified gluten-free, but oats are not in the spec's restriction code list. Soy sauce can contain gluten, but tamari (the noted alternative) typically does not — canonical choice is soy sauce. Conservative default: soy only. Flagged for operator review if gluten should be added.
_fixture_notes.cuisine: Harissa, rose harissa, North African spicing — characteristically Moroccan/North African in flavour profile. 'moroccan' assigned consistent with the spec's worked example for a similarly spiced dish. Flagged for operator confirmation.
_fixture_notes.cook_time_minutes: Source states 'Active time: ~25 mins Total: ~1 hr 15 mins'. Active time used as prep_time_minutes (labelled field). Total is a combined total — spec prohibits deriving cook time by subtraction. Cook time derived instead from stated step durations. Parallel block: step 1 (potatoes, 18 min), step 3 (air fry, 14 min), step 4 (sweat onion, 8 min) run concurrently — source explicitly signals this ('While the veg are in the air fryer'). Critical path of parallel block = 18 min. Sequential base steps: step 5 (90 sec) + step 6 (5 min) + step 7 (18 min) = 24.5 min. Bake: 25 min. Total critical path: 18 + 24.5 + 25 = 67.5 → rounded to 68 min. Post-cook rest (step 13, 5 min) excluded. Steps 8–11 are active hands-on with no stated durations; not added. cook_time_minutes: 68.
_fixture_notes.prep_time_minutes: Labelled 'Active time: ~25 mins'. Used at face value. prep_time_minutes: 25.
_fixture_notes.olive_oil_triplicate: Olive oil appears in three distinct uses: 3 tbsp for the mash (0003), 2 tbsp for air-fried veg (0009), 3 tbsp for the base (0013). Three separate rows per duplicate-ingredient-across-groups convention.
_fixture_notes.ground_cumin_duplicate: Ground cumin appears in two distinct groups: 0.5 tsp for air-fried veg (0011) and 2 tsp for the base (0016). Separate rows.
_fixture_notes.smoked_paprika_duplicate: Smoked paprika appears in two distinct groups: 0.5 tsp for air-fried veg (0012) and 2 tsp for the base (0018). Separate rows.
_fixture_notes.harissa_range: Source lists '3 tbsp rose harissa' with instruction to start with 2 tbsp if the harissa is fiercer. This is cooking guidance rather than a strict range quantity — the canonical amount is 3 tbsp. Amount set to 3, note records the lower-heat guidance. Flagged: could be interpreted as a range (2–3 tbsp) under the range convention, in which case amount would be 2. Conservative default: use stated amount of 3 with note. Operator to review.
_fixture_notes.inline_alternatives: Five inline-alternative cases: (1) soy sauce/tamari → soy sauce canonical; (2) red/yellow split lentils → red canonical; (3) white/brown miso → white canonical; (4) sherry vinegar/red wine vinegar → sherry vinegar canonical; (5) parsley/coriander → parsley canonical. All per inline-alternatives convention.
_fixture_notes.maris_piper: 'Maris Piper or King Edward' are variety qualifiers on floury potatoes, not ingredient alternatives — they are shopping guidance. Not treated as inline alternatives; folded into ingredient notes.
_fixture_notes.salt_and_pepper: Source lists 'Salt and black pepper' as a combined line under mash ingredients. Combined-seasonings convention: split into two rows (0005 salt, 0006 black pepper). Both referenced in mash steps and step 4 (pinch of salt for sweating onion).
_fixture_notes.fresh_parsley: Source lists 'Small handful of fresh parsley or coriander, chopped'. Inline-alternatives convention: parsley canonical. 'Chopped' is a prep verb — trailing form: 'fresh parsley, chopped'. 'Fresh' is canonical-identity modifier, stays. Amount null (small handful is a non-standard quantity); notes record 'small handful'.
_fixture_notes.no_source: Source is an operator-written document with no external attribution. source field left null.
_fixture_notes.no_derived_components: Batch mode — no derived components proposed. The lentil and chickpea base is a strong component_dinner/base derivation candidate, closely analogous to the north-african-spiced-lentil-bean-base in the spec's worked example. The air-fried veg could also be a component_dinner/veg candidate. Both flagged for operator review.
